This page shows how the embedded screenshots fit together as a working Notion setup for Heart-Strong Midwifery: One place to track every Missoula family through primary midwifery care — consult, intake + 3-day nutritional form, monthly/biweekly/every-few-days prenatal cadence, labs, on-call window, and the postpartum + newborn arc.

What this is

A preview of a duplicate-ready Notion client tracker organized around the public workflow visible on the site.

Public workflow used

primary midwifery care, 3-day nutritional intake form, endless amounts of calls, texts, and emails, monthly comprehensive prenatal appointments

Today ops snapshot Prenatal + birth + postpartum board Family record — Giselle K. Consult + intake lane Prenatal cadence + on-call window Postpartum visit + newborn exam Setup map

Why this shape

From overview to usable workspace

The first screenshots show the main views. The later screenshots show the editable structure that would be delivered: fields, focused tables, individual client pages, and starter records.

What it helps with

  • Every family tracked from first call through 6-week postpartum — replaces the 'calls, texts, and emails' pile with one page
  • Prenatal cadence (monthly to 28 -> biweekly 28-36 -> every few days 36.5+ -> 41-42wk post-dates) built in, not retyped each pregnancy
  • Labs + scans (10-12wk bloods, 20wk anatomy, 28wk GDM + anemia, 36.5wk GBS) tracked per family with result-received state
